question:
Why is the norm of "reciprocal self-disclosure" — matching the level of personal openness another person shows you — important in building trust?
A) Sharing equal amounts of information is required by law in formal relationships.
B) It signals mutual investment and creates a balanced vulnerability that allows both parties to feel safe opening up further.
C) It ensures that neither person ever has to share anything truly private.
D) People who disclose more than their partner are considered more trustworthy.
